description: PR-10.4a semantic-variant model + validators review (companion to pr10-4a-semantic-variants.md).
method: operator adversarial self-audit of variants.py + item-variant.schema.json plus a test suite, THEN an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The reviewer read variants.py, the schema, factstore/quiz/presentation, the tests, and scaffold_validate, and empirically confirmed the key defects (wrong-choice-graded-correct, learner-render concept leak, None-bypass of the drift gate).
independent_verdict: round1 needs_work -> all 9 fixed; round2 (focused re-review of the redesign) accept_with_fixes -> all 4 fixed. Committed after both.

re_review.method: a second INDEPENDENT agent focused-re-review verified each of F1-F9 is genuinely fixed and hunted for NEW soundness/leakage defects introduced by the isolated-key redesign. It confirmed all 9 fixed (incl. rebuilding the F1 attack and confirming it is now rejected, and probing the render for must_test/correct_concept leakage). Verdict accept_with_fixes; no overstated claims remained.

new_findings.problem: the default holdout-id load in gate 2 was unguarded, so a corrupt holdout store would crash the validator instead of returning a fail-closed gate error (inconsistent with validate_gold).
new_findings.resolution: wrapped in try/except -> returns a clean 'holdout store unreadable' gate error; uses h.get('id').

new_findings.problem: variant_key.rationale_refs was not checked as a subset of the variant's fact_ids (gold enforces this).

the lock corroboration proves the keyed choice uniquely carries the keyword, not semantic correctness (an author can still key a wrong index — the answer-key author-trust model). Not a regression; docstrings already hedged.
new_findings.resolution: added an explicit doc note that automated gates cannot verify semantic correctness and human review (status=='reviewed'+metadata) is the backstop.

new_findings.problem: the bare-letter correct_concept guard only caught A-D/a-d/digit, missing E/F for 5-6 choice items.
new_findings.resolution: broadened to reject any single-character correct_concept; test_correct_concept_bare_letter_E_is_rejected.

independent_findings.problem: the 'semantic_lock is the key' model only checked that exactly one choice contained the must_include_keywords; it never verified that choice was the intended correct answer. A grounded lock keyword landing uniquely on a WRONG choice validated as [] and grade_variant marked the wrong choice correct — a direct mis-grading vector once API-authored variants arrive.
independent_findings.resolution: correctness moved to a SEPARATE evaluator-context variant key (correct_index), mirroring answer-key isolation. The semantic_lock now only CORROBORATES the key: lock_consistency_problems requires the keyed-correct choice to contain all must_include_keywords and NO other choice to (no second defensible answer). grade_variant keys off the isolated correct_index, never the keyword. Regressions: test_keyword_on_wrong_choice_is_rejected, test_second_choice_satisfying_lock_is_rejected, test_variant_grading_uses_the_key_not_the_keyword.

independent_findings.problem: _synthetic_item set topic = semantic_lock.must_test (schema-required, always overrides). topic is in quiz.LEARNER_FIELDS, so render_variant projected the answer-adjacent must_test straight into the learner render — defeating the leakage-safe render even though the field NAME allowlist passed.
independent_findings.resolution: _synthetic_item uses the SOURCE item's neutral topic; must_test stays validation-only. Test test_render_does_not_leak_must_test_or_correct_concept asserts neither must_test nor correct_concept appears in the render and topic == source topic.

independent_findings.problem: the misconception-drift clause ran only 'if source_misconception_ids is not None', and validate_variant defaulted it to None — so the documented signature silently skipped gate 13's misconception check with no signal.
independent_findings.resolution: targets are derived from the source item's isolated key (_source_misconception_ids(source_key)); a missing/malformed source key fails closed ('cannot verify misconception drift'). Negative test test_missing_source_key_fails_closed_on_drift added.

independent_findings.problem: status=='validated' returned eligible unconditionally, so a self-asserted 'validated' (a draft flipped without running the validator) was treated as practice-eligible.
independent_findings.resolution: is_practice_eligible now requires status 'reviewed' AND reviewer metadata only; bare 'validated'/'draft' are not eligible. Test test_bare_validated_is_not_practice_eligible added.

independent_findings.problem: correct_concept was never validated (could be a bare letter 'B') and the lock hash was described as a drift signal but compared to nothing.
independent_findings.resolution: correct_concept is rejected if empty or a bare letter/index; semantic_lock_hash re-described as a lineage/traceability id (not a live drift gate). Test test_correct_concept_bare_letter_is_rejected added.

independent_findings.problem: nothing validated a variant against the schema, so additionalProperties:false was never exercised; the code only checked a fixed 10-name denylist, leaving arbitrarily-named smuggle fields (e.g. 'solution') uncaught.
independent_findings.resolution: replaced the denylist with a closed-object ALLOWLIST check (unknown_field_problems) over top-level and semantic_lock keys, so any field of any name outside the schema's allowed set fails. Tests test_unknown_smuggle_field_is_rejected, test_unknown_lock_field_is_rejected added.

independent_findings.problem: several negatives asserted weak substrings that an unrelated problem could satisfy (e.g. any('objective' in p) matched both the drift message and a gate-6 message), and the most dangerous failure modes had no test.
independent_findings.resolution: assertions tightened to specific messages ('drifts from source', 'introduce a claim not in the source'); F1/F2/F3/F6 negatives added; the valid case asserts == [].

independent_findings.problem: gate 2 relied only on the caller-supplied source_item's holdout flag; validate_variant never consulted the holdout store.
independent_findings.resolution: validate_variant now also cross-checks source_item_id against the holdout id set (loaded when not supplied), so a mislabeled source cannot slip through.

independent_findings.problem: the too-similar bound (0.85) was looser than the goldset's own near-duplicate standard (quiz._NEAR_DUP_THRESHOLD, 0.75), so a 'variant' could be closer to its source than two goldset stems may be.

clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: grounding reuse is real, not bypassed: _synthetic_item sets source_policy 'factstore|required' so factstore.validate_item runs and checks fact-id existence/active/bank-eligibility + expected_keywords; bank drift double-caught (gate 3 + gate 5)
determinism: semantic_lock_hash and presentation ordering are deterministic (sha256, sorted, stable sort, no random/clock)
grade_variant fails closed on malformed key / presentation_id mismatch / non-int/out-of-range displayed index
variant_lineage is content-free with readiness_weight pinned to 0; gate 1 and gate 8 correctly enforced
scaffold count gate 7->8 is the only needed change; the new schema carries schema_version + migration_policy; scaffold runs green
the valid fixture is grounded for the right reasons (fact card contains the lock keyword; the misconception resolves at the source objective)
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson) and returned needs_work with three MAJOR soundness defects — including a variant that graded the wrong choice correct and a learner-render leak. All nine findings were fixed before commit (the module was redesigned around an isolated variant key). This is the review process working as intended: the defects were caught pre-commit, not shipped.
